entities.name: Bleeding
entries: If you have the Bleeding condition, you cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} except by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The condition ends when a creature finishes a {@variantrule Alternative Rest System|GrimHollowCG24|Quick}, {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}

entities.name: Corruption
entries: While you have the Corruption condition, you experience the following effects.

entries.name: Heroic Corruption
entries: You cannot have {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} or give it to another player character who lacks it.

entries.name: Corruption Levels
entries: This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 Corruption level. If your Corruption level is 6, you either die or transform into a creature of the GM's choosing, under their control.

entries.name: Removing Corruption Levels
entries: The {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB}, {@spell Heal|XPHB}, or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell removes all of your Corruption levels and ends the condition.

entities.name: Dazed
entries: If you have the Dazed condition, you cannot maintain {@condition Concentration|XPHB}.
